title: Sentinel Outbound Event
description: Events Sentinel emits to Anoryx-AI-Orchestrator (and onward to Delta) over the Redis Streams bus. Every variant carries the four stable IDs from contracts/ids.md (LOCKED, IMMUTABLE) plus event_id, event_timestamp, and request_id. These IDs are the join key between Sentinel events and Delta records. NORMATIVE DISPATCH: variant selection is determined SOLELY by oneOf plus the event_type const on each variant; the const values are unique and the variants are mutually exclusive, so exactly one matches a valid record. VALIDATOR DIALECT: Sentinel, Anoryx-AI-Orchestrator, and Delta MUST all validate these records with a JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 validator so no parser-differential exists. All objects are closed (additionalProperties:false) and all fields are bounded (maxLength/maximum/maxItems) per the F-001 audit findings: closed schemas remove a silent smuggling channel and bounded fields remove a DoS-via-inspection vector. Attribution carried on events is always the server-resolved value (see contracts/openapi.yaml virtual-API-key key-to-ID binding and id_context_mismatch), never a raw client-supplied header.
$comment: Dispatch is normative via oneOf + the event_type const on each variant under JSON Schema Draft 2020-12. The 'discriminator' keyword below is a NON-NORMATIVE hint for OpenAPI tooling ONLY; it is not a 2020-12 keyword and has NO validation effect under this dialect. Do not rely on it for correctness.

tenant_id.description: Stable tenant identifier (UUID v4). LOCKED/IMMUTABLE per contracts/ids.md. Server-resolved value, not a raw client header.

team_id.description: Stable team identifier (UUID v4). LOCKED/IMMUTABLE per contracts/ids.md.

project_id.description: Stable project identifier (UUID v4). LOCKED/IMMUTABLE per contracts/ids.md.

agent_id.description: Internal Sentinel component name (lowercase slug). LOCKED/IMMUTABLE per contracts/ids.md. NOT the end-user AI model name.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this event (UUID v4). Deduplication key on the bus.

event_timestamp.description: Event emission time (RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 date-time, UTC).

request_id.description: Correlation ID matching the originating gateway request (X-Request-Id in contracts/openapi.yaml). Links the event back to the request that produced it. Charset is restricted to [A-Za-z0-9._-] to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense).

actor_id.description: OPTIONAL honest per-operator attribution carrier (F-014 / ADR-0017 §10, vector 16). Holds the INTERNAL admin_users.id — an opaque UUID string (VARCHAR(64)-bounded, mirroring event_id's shape) — joinable (RLS-scoped) to the operator identity. It is the ONLY event field that names a specific HUMAN operator, while agent_id names the emitting subsystem ('operator-sso'/'admin-console'). It is NEVER PII: never the raw IdP subject, email, NameID, or any credential (R6). REQUIRED on operator_sso_login (a successful login always has a provisioned admin_users row); OPTIONAL/nullable elsewhere — absent on operator_sso_denied (a denied login may have no provisioned user), absent on admin_breakglass_used (the env-token break-glass has no operator identity), and absent on idp_config_changed when performed via break-glass. When omitted, the field is simply not present (additionalProperties:false keeps every variant closed).

UsageEvent.description: Per-request token/cost/latency usage record. Drives the Delta client-side cost estimate; cost_estimate_cents is an estimate, not an authoritative bill.

model.description: End-user AI model name reported by the gateway (e.g. the value sent in the request body).

cost_estimate_cents.description: Client-side cost estimate in cents. Estimate only; not an authoritative bill.

PiiBlockedEvent.description: Emitted when the data-protection engine detects and acts on PII.

pattern_name.description: Name of the PII detection pattern that matched (e.g. 'us_ssn', 'email'). Never the matched value.

severity.description: Severity of the detected PII exposure.

action_taken.description: Mitigation the gateway applied.

sample_excerpt_redacted.description: OPTIONAL already-redacted excerpt for audit context. This field is intended to be ALREADY REDACTED by the data-protection engine before emission; it MUST NEVER contain raw PII or the matched value. The pattern is a MARKER-PRESENCE check ONLY: it requires a redaction marker ('[REDACTED]' or '***') somewhere in the value. It does NOT guarantee the ABSENCE of raw PII and provides NO structural prevention of raw PII riding alongside a marker. Full PII suppression is the emitter's runtime obligation, not a schema guarantee. Consumers MUST treat this field as potentially-unsafe display data requiring consumer-side validation before rendering or storage. Emitters that cannot guarantee redaction MUST omit this field.

InjectionDetectedEvent.description: Emitted when the defense engine flags a prompt-injection attempt.

rule_matched.description: Identifier of the detection rule that matched. Never the offending input text.

action_taken.description: Mitigation the gateway applied.

SecretLeakedEvent.description: Emitted when the defense engine detects a secret in traffic.

secret_type.description: Classification of the detected secret. The secret value itself is NEVER emitted.

direction.description: Traffic direction in which the secret was detected (inbound to model vs outbound to client).

action_taken.description: Mitigation the gateway applied.

PolicyViolatedEvent.description: Emitted when a Delta-sourced policy (contracts/policy.schema.json) is violated at the gateway.

policy_id.description: Identifier of the violated policy (UUID v4); joins to contracts/policy.schema.json policy_id.

violation_type.description: Classification of the violation (e.g. 'budget_exceeded', 'model_denied'). Charset is restricted to [A-Za-z0-9._-] to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense).

action_taken.description: Enforcement the gateway applied.

ComplianceCheckedEvent.description: Emitted when a compliance control is evaluated for audit-ready evidence. 'status' reports the result of an individual control check; it does NOT assert certification or overall compliance.

framework.description: Audit framework the control belongs to.

control_id.description: Framework-specific control identifier. Charset is restricted to [A-Za-z0-9._-] to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense).

status.description: Result of this individual control check. Audit-ready evidence, not a certification claim.

ShadowAiDetectedEvent.description: Emitted when traffic to an unsanctioned (shadow) AI endpoint is observed.

detected_endpoint.description: PII-SAFETY: host (and optionally path) of the detected shadow AI endpoint ONLY. Emitters MUST NOT log full URLs: query strings and fragments may carry API keys, tokens, or PII. Strip query params, fragments, and userinfo before emission. Structurally enforced: the value MUST NOT contain '?', '#', '@', or whitespace, so query strings, fragments, and userinfo cannot ride along. Bounded to 256 chars to cap inspection cost.

traffic_volume.description: Observed request count to the shadow endpoint over the detection window.

first_seen_at.description: When traffic to this endpoint was first observed (RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 date-time, UTC).

RoutingDecisionEvent.description: Emitted by the multi-provider model router (F-006, ADR-0008) when it makes a routing decision: provider selection, allow-list denial, cost-ceiling block, a fallback attempt, or chain exhaustion. NOT a Delta policy violation (that is policy_violated, reserved per ADR-0007). agent_id is the emitting component slug 'gateway-core' (the router runs inside the gateway), NEVER a provider or model name; the provider is carried in selected_provider. No provider credential, API key, or upstream body text is EVER carried on this event.

routing_reason.description: Lowercase slug for why this decision was made (e.g. 'tenant-allowlist', 'cost-routing', 'fallback-transient', 'fallback-rate-limit'). Charset restricted to a slug to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense).

selected_provider.description: The upstream provider chosen for THIS routing decision. Never a model name; never a credential.

outcome.description: The routing outcome for this decision.

attempt_index.description: Zero-based index of this attempt in the fallback chain (0 = primary). Bounded to 16 to cap inspection cost (DoS-via-inspection defense).

requested_model.description: The model the client requested (echo for correlation). Never a secret.

action_taken.description: Disposition the router applied: 'routed' (served by selected_provider), 'blocked' (allow-list/cost terminal), 'failed_over' (retryable failure, advancing the fallback chain).

PolicyIntakeAcceptedEvent.description: Emitted by F-008 policy intake (ADR-0009) when a Delta-signed policy record is verified, scope-resolved (the verified signature claims are authoritative; body IDs are a cross-check only), version-fresh, persisted, and audited. agent_id is the emitting component slug 'gateway-core'. Carries NO signature material, key material, or raw policy plaintext; policy_id is a join hint to contracts/policy.schema.json.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (the verified policy was recorded).

policy_id.description: Identifier of the accepted policy; joins to contracts/policy.schema.json policy_id.

PolicyIntakeRejectedSignatureEvent.description: Emitted by F-008 policy intake when signature verification fails (absent/malformed/wrong-alg/wrong-key) or no verifying key is configured (fail-closed). No tenant is resolvable, so the four IDs are the reserved system-sentinel values (zero-UUID tenant/team/project, 'gateway-core' agent) per ADR-0009 Decision B. Carries NO signature bytes.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'blocked' (record rejected, not persisted).

violation_type.description: Reason slug (e.g. 'signature.invalid', 'signature.no_key'). Charset restricted to forbid control chars/whitespace (log-injection defense).

PolicyIntakeRejectedScopeMismatchEvent.description: Emitted by F-008 policy intake when the verified signature scope disagrees with the record body, or the signature claims the forbidden wildcard tenant. Attributed to the signature-resolved tenant when one exists, else the system-sentinel IDs (ADR-0009 Decision B). violation_type carries ONLY the mismatched-dimension slug — the raw disputed body IDs are NEVER emitted (they go to structured logs keyed by request_id).

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'blocked'.

policy_id.description: Identifier of the policy, when resolvable; joins to contracts/policy.schema.json policy_id.

violation_type.description: Mismatched-dimension slug (e.g. 'scope_mismatch.tenant_id', 'scope_mismatch.wildcard_tenant'). Never the disputed raw IDs.

PolicyIntakeRejectedReplayEvent.description: Emitted by F-008 policy intake when policy_version is not strictly greater than the stored max for the policy_id (replay/rollback defense). The signature is valid, so it is attributed to the signature-resolved tenant.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'blocked'.

policy_id.description: Identifier of the replayed policy; joins to contracts/policy.schema.json policy_id.

violation_type.description: Reason slug (e.g. 'replay'). No stored-version value is leaked here.

PolicyIntakeRejectedSchemaEvent.description: Emitted by F-008 policy intake when the record fails Draft 2020-12 schema validation (unparseable, oversized, missing field, additionalProperties poisoning, wrong variant). No signature is parsed, so it is attributed to the system-sentinel IDs (ADR-0009 Decision B).

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'blocked'.

PolicyDecisionAllowEvent.description: Emitted at request time (F-006 router, F-008 enforcement) when a model allow-list policy permitted the requested model for the request scope. agent_id is 'gateway-core'. NOT a Delta policy violation (that is policy_violated).

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (request permitted).

policy_id.description: Identifier of the allow-list policy that applied; joins to contracts/policy.schema.json policy_id.

requested_model.description: The model the client requested (echo). Never a secret.

PolicyDecisionDenyEvent.description: Emitted at request time (F-006 router, F-008 enforcement) when a model deny-list (deny precedence over allow), an absent-from-allow-list condition, or a budget ceiling terminally blocked the request for the scope. agent_id is 'gateway-core'. NOT a Delta policy violation (that is policy_violated).

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'blocked' (request terminally denied, no fallback).

policy_id.description: Identifier of the policy that denied the request; joins to contracts/policy.schema.json policy_id.

requested_model.description: The model the client requested (echo). Never a secret.

PromptInjectionDetectedMlEvent.description: Emitted by F-007 (ADR-0010) when the LLM-as-judge injection classifier produced a usable verdict for a prompt. final_score is the fused score = max(classifier_score regex component, judge_score). agent_id is the emitting component slug 'gateway-core'; judge_model is the judge's model name, NEVER a credential. No raw prompt text is carried: rule_matched is a stable rule id and the offending input is never emitted.

classifier_score.description: Normalized injection-likelihood score in [0,1] from the regex/heuristic component.

judge_score.description: Normalized injection-likelihood score in [0,1] from the LLM judge verdict.

judge_confidence.description: Judge-reported confidence in its verdict, normalized to [0,1].

judge_model.description: Judge model name (e.g. 'claude-haiku-4-5'). Never a credential.

final_score.description: Fused decision score in [0,1] = max(classifier_score, judge_score).

audit_mode.description: Audit-evidence mode in effect when this verdict was recorded.

rule_matched.description: OPTIONAL stable identifier of the detection rule that contributed. Never the offending input text.

action_taken.description: Mitigation the gateway applied.

ClassifierUnconfiguredEvent.description: Emitted by F-007 (ADR-0010) when the LLM-as-judge classifier is not configured (e.g. no preset/model wired) and therefore could not be invoked for a prompt. Detect-and-audit only: the gateway records the gap; it is NOT an injection verdict. agent_id is 'gateway-core'. No raw prompt text or credential is carried.

classifier_reason.description: OPTIONAL lowercase slug for why the classifier was unconfigured (e.g. 'no_preset'). Charset restricted to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense).

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (detect-and-audit only).

ClassifierDegradedEvent.description: Emitted by F-007 (ADR-0010) when the LLM-as-judge classifier ran in a degraded mode (e.g. partial/timed-out verdict that could not be fused into a usable score). Detect-and-audit only: it is NOT an injection verdict. agent_id is 'gateway-core'. No raw prompt text or credential is carried.

classifier_reason.description: OPTIONAL lowercase slug for why the classifier was degraded (e.g. 'partial_verdict'). Charset restricted to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense).

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (detect-and-audit only).

ClassifierInvocationFailedEvent.description: Emitted by F-007 (ADR-0010) when invoking the LLM-as-judge classifier failed outright (e.g. transport error, upstream error, no response). Detect-and-audit only: it is NOT an injection verdict. agent_id is 'gateway-core'. No raw prompt text or credential is carried.

classifier_reason.description: OPTIONAL lowercase slug for why invocation failed (e.g. 'upstream_error', 'timeout'). Charset restricted to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense).

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (detect-and-audit only).

ShadowAiDetectedOutboundEvent.description: Emitted by the F-007 (ADR-0010) egress monitor when outbound traffic targets a disallowed AI provider endpoint (shadow-AI egress). Detect-and-audit only: the monitor does NOT block. agent_id is 'gateway-core'; selected_provider is the disallowed provider the egress targeted, never a credential.

detected_endpoint.description: PII-SAFETY: host (and optionally path) of the detected shadow AI endpoint ONLY. Emitters MUST NOT log full URLs: query strings and fragments may carry API keys, tokens, or PII. Strip query params, fragments, and userinfo before emission. Structurally enforced: the value MUST NOT contain '?', '#', '@', or whitespace, so query strings, fragments, and userinfo cannot ride along. Bounded to 256 chars to cap inspection cost.

traffic_volume.description: Observed request count to the shadow endpoint over the detection window.

first_seen_at.description: When traffic to this endpoint was first observed (RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 date-time, UTC).

selected_provider.description: The disallowed upstream provider the egress targeted. Never a model name; never a credential.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (detect-and-audit only; the monitor does not block).

RecursiveInjectionAttemptEvent.description: Emitted by F-007 (ADR-0010) when a prompt is detected to target the classifier surface itself (a recursive/meta injection aimed at the judge). agent_id is 'gateway-core'. No raw prompt text is carried: rule_matched is a stable rule id and the offending input is never emitted.

rule_matched.description: OPTIONAL stable identifier of the detection rule that matched. Never the offending input text.

action_taken.description: Mitigation the gateway applied.

JudgeBillingEvent.description: Emitted by F-007 (ADR-0010) as a cost-audit trail of a single LLM-as-judge invocation. cost_estimate_cents is a CLIENT-SIDE COST ESTIMATE, not an authoritative bill. agent_id is 'gateway-core'; selected_provider/judge_model/judge_preset are the judge provider and model, never credentials. No raw prompt text is carried.

judge_preset.description: Judge preset identifier (e.g. 'anthropic:claude-haiku-4-5'). Never a credential.

judge_model.description: Judge model name. Never a credential.

selected_provider.description: The judge upstream provider. Never a model name; never a credential.

cost_estimate_cents.description: Client-side cost estimate in cents for this judge invocation. Estimate only; not an authoritative bill.

judge_outcome.description: Outcome of the judge invocation being billed.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (cost-audit trail).

RateLimitDegradedEvent.description: Emitted by F-009 (ADR-0011 §7) when the Redis-backed rate limiter falls back to the in-process limiter on a Redis connection/timeout failure (failure mode γ). Detect-and-audit only: enforcement continues per-worker on the legacy path; this event records the degradation transition. Emitted with the triggering request's REAL four IDs when raised on the admission path; emitted with the reserved system IDs (tenant/team/project = WILDCARD_UUID, agent_id = 'rate-limiter') when raised by the background health loop (see contracts/ids.md, third documented WILDCARD_UUID use). Carries ONLY the Redis error class/module for forensics — NEVER the exception message, which may contain a host, port, or credential.

redis_error_class.description: OPTIONAL Redis exception class name (e.g. 'ConnectionError', 'TimeoutError') from type(exc).__name__. Forensic only. NEVER the exception message (it may leak a host/port/credential).

redis_error_module.description: OPTIONAL module of the Redis exception class (e.g. 'redis.exceptions') from type(exc).__module__. Forensic only. NEVER the exception message.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (detect-and-audit only; the limiter continues enforcing on the in-process fallback path).

RateLimitRecoveredEvent.description: Emitted by F-009 (ADR-0011 §7) when the background health loop observes Redis returning to health and the limiter resumes the Redis-backed primary path (failure mode γ recovery). Detect-and-audit only. Always a system-emitted event with no request context, so it carries the reserved system IDs (tenant/team/project = WILDCARD_UUID, agent_id = 'rate-limiter'); see contracts/ids.md (third documented WILDCARD_UUID use). tenant_id = WILDCARD_UUID here denotes 'the Sentinel system itself', never a cross-tenant grant.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (detect-and-audit only; the Redis-backed primary path has resumed).

RateLimitRedisErrorEvent.description: Emitted by F-009 (ADR-0011 §7) on the admission path per distinct Redis error encountered by the rate limiter (forensic trail, distinct from the once-per-outage rate_limit_degraded transition). Detect-and-audit only: enforcement continues per-worker on the legacy path. Carries the triggering request's REAL four IDs. Carries ONLY the Redis error class/module for forensics — NEVER the exception message, which may contain a host, port, or credential.

redis_error_class.description: OPTIONAL Redis exception class name (e.g. 'ConnectionError', 'TimeoutError') from type(exc).__name__. Forensic only. NEVER the exception message (it may leak a host/port/credential).

redis_error_module.description: OPTIONAL module of the Redis exception class (e.g. 'redis.exceptions') from type(exc).__module__. Forensic only. NEVER the exception message.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (detect-and-audit only; the limiter continues enforcing on the in-process fallback path).

ComplianceEvidenceGeneratedEvent.description: Emitted by F-011 (ADR-0013) after a tenant-scoped compliance evidence summary is generated over events_audit_log for a window. agent_id is the emitting component slug 'compliance-engine'. Carries the caller's REAL four IDs (a tenant generating evidence over its own data, NOT WILDCARD_UUID). Metadata only — NO payloads, prompt content, PII, or secrets. framework/framework_version describe which framework mapping was evaluated. This is audit-ready evidence, not a certification claim.

framework.description: Audit framework the evidence summary was generated for. v1 supports SOC2 and ISO27001 only. NOTE: this enum is local to the F-011 compliance meta-event variants and is deliberately distinct from ComplianceCheckedEvent.framework (which is unchanged).

framework_version.description: Pinned framework mapping version copied verbatim from the version-controlled mapping YAML (e.g. '2017-TSC-rev2022'). Charset restricted to [A-Za-z0-9._-] to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense).

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (a meta-audit record of an evidence-summary generation; the generation path itself issues zero writes to events_audit_log).

CompliancePackExportedEvent.description: Emitted by F-011 (ADR-0013) after a tenant-scoped, ECDSA-signed compliance evidence pack ZIP is exported. agent_id is the emitting component slug 'compliance-engine'. Carries the caller's REAL four IDs (a tenant exporting evidence over its own data, NOT WILDCARD_UUID). Metadata only — NO payloads, prompt content, PII, or secrets. pack_content_hash is the forensic linkage to the exported ZIP. This is audit-ready evidence, not a certification claim.

framework.description: Audit framework the exported pack was generated for. v1 supports SOC2 and ISO27001 only. NOTE: this enum is local to the F-011 compliance meta-event variants and is deliberately distinct from ComplianceCheckedEvent.framework (which is unchanged).

framework_version.description: Pinned framework mapping version copied verbatim from the version-controlled mapping YAML (e.g. '2017-TSC-rev2022'). Charset restricted to [A-Za-z0-9._-] to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense).

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (a meta-audit record of a pack export).

pack_content_hash.description: SHA-256 hex of the canonical signed pack (forensic linkage to the exported ZIP). Never a payload.

AdminTenantCreatedEvent.description: Emitted by F-012a (ADR-0014 §8) when the admin/operator principal creates a tenant. Honest admin attribution: agent_id is the reserved slug 'admin-console' (the operator principal, NOT the system and NOT the target tenant); tenant_id is the TARGET tenant created; team_id = project_id = WILDCARD_UUID because the action is tenant-level, not scoped to a team/project (contracts/ids.md 4th documented WILDCARD_UUID use). Metadata only — NO secrets, key material, or payloads.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (admin meta-audit record; no engine bypass).

AdminTenantDeactivatedEvent.description: Emitted by F-012a (ADR-0014 §8) when the admin/operator principal soft-deactivates a tenant (is_active=false; no hard delete, the audit chain survives). Honest admin attribution: agent_id is the reserved slug 'admin-console'; tenant_id is the TARGET tenant; team_id = project_id = WILDCARD_UUID (tenant-level action; contracts/ids.md 4th documented WILDCARD_UUID use). Metadata only — NO secrets, key material, or payloads.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (admin meta-audit record; soft-deactivate, audit chain untouched).

AdminKeyMintedEvent.description: Emitted by F-012a (ADR-0014 §8) when the admin/operator principal mints a virtual key (including the new key on a rotation). Honest admin attribution: agent_id is the reserved slug 'admin-console'; tenant_id is the TARGET tenant; team_id/project_id are the key's REAL team/project (known at mint), NOT WILDCARD_UUID. Metadata only — the plaintext key secret and HMAC fingerprint are NEVER carried on the event (secret is surfaced once in the API response only).

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (admin meta-audit record; key secret never carried).

AdminKeyRevokedEvent.description: Emitted by F-012a (ADR-0014 §8) when the admin/operator principal revokes a virtual key (including the old key on a rotation; rotation is immediate-revoke). Honest admin attribution: agent_id is the reserved slug 'admin-console'; tenant_id is the TARGET tenant; team_id/project_id are the key's REAL team/project, NOT WILDCARD_UUID. Metadata only — NO key secret or fingerprint is carried.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (admin meta-audit record; key secret never carried).

AdminConfigUpdatedEvent.description: Emitted by F-012a (ADR-0014 §8) when the admin/operator principal adjusts tenant routing config (classifier_model_id / audit_mode / team_rpm_limit) via a bounded UPDATE constrained by the table's existing CHECK constraints — config data only, NO engine logic changed. Honest admin attribution: agent_id is the reserved slug 'admin-console'; tenant_id is the TARGET tenant; team_id = project_id = WILDCARD_UUID (tenant-level action; contracts/ids.md 4th documented WILDCARD_UUID use). Metadata only — NO secrets or payloads.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (admin meta-audit record; bounded config update, engine logic untouched).

AdminAuditAccessedEvent.description: Emitted by F-012a (ADR-0014 §8) on every admin/operator cross-tenant READ (audit log, policy status, config view, or compliance evidence over a target tenant). The audit-read serving query itself writes zero rows (ADR-0014 §9 R1/R5 reconciliation); this access event is a separate, explicit append. Honest admin attribution: agent_id is the reserved slug 'admin-console'; tenant_id is the TARGET tenant read; team_id = project_id = WILDCARD_UUID (tenant-level action; contracts/ids.md 4th documented WILDCARD_UUID use). Metadata only — NO read payloads, PII, or secrets.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (admin meta-audit record of a cross-tenant read; the serving query writes zero rows).

OperatorSsoLoginEvent.description: Emitted by F-014 (ADR-0017 §10, D9) when an SSO operator login succeeds (assertion validated, group->role resolved, operator-session minted). Honest attribution: agent_id is the reserved slug 'operator-sso' (the SSO subsystem principal — NOT the system, NOT the target tenant), and actor_id names the SPECIFIC operator (the admin_users.id). tenant_id is the operator's OWN tenant (a real tenant UUID — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID, since an operator login is always bound to one tenant via the idp_config audience/issuer->tenant binding); team_id = project_id = WILDCARD_UUID because the action is tenant-level, not scoped to a team/project (contracts/ids.md WILDCARD_UUID use). Metadata only — NO IdP subject/email/NameID, NO assertion bytes, NO secrets, NO operator-session material.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (SSO operator-login meta-audit record).

OperatorSsoDeniedEvent.description: Emitted by F-014 (ADR-0017 §10, D9) when an SSO assertion validated but the subject was denied — its IdP group(s) map to no role (fail-closed, D6) or the subject is unknown/unprovisioned (threat vector 14). The subject is NOT provisioned, so there is NO admin_users row and actor_id is absent/nullable. Honest attribution: agent_id is the reserved slug 'operator-sso'. tenant_id is the tenant owning the matched idp_config when resolvable; when the failure is pre-binding (no tenant resolves) tenant_id = WILDCARD_UUID — the documented system-scoped-audit-owner use (a SYSTEM attribution, never a cross-tenant grant). team_id = project_id = WILDCARD_UUID (tenant-level action). Metadata only — NO IdP subject/email/NameID, NO assertion bytes, NO secrets.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'blocked' (the subject was denied and not provisioned; fail-closed group->role, D6).

AdminBreakglassUsedEvent.description: Emitted by F-014 (ADR-0017 §10, D9 / D7) when the env-token break-glass path authenticates (the cross-tenant recovery/bootstrap path, kept and audited distinctly from operator_sso_login). The break-glass token has NO operator identity, so actor_id is absent/nullable. This is a SYSTEM-scoped auth event with no single target tenant, so tenant_id = WILDCARD_UUID (a SYSTEM attribution per contracts/ids.md, NEVER a cross-tenant grant); team_id = project_id = WILDCARD_UUID. Honest attribution: agent_id is the reserved slug 'admin-console' (the break-glass principal, unchanged from F-012a). Metadata only — NO token bytes, NO secrets.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (break-glass auth meta-audit record; always visible in the audit log, D7).

IdpConfigChangedEvent.description: Emitted by F-014 (ADR-0017 §10, D9 / D3) when an operator creates, updates, or rotates a tenant's IdP configuration. Honest attribution: agent_id is the acting principal slug ('operator-sso' for an SSO-authenticated operator, or 'admin-console' for the break-glass path); actor_id names the SPECIFIC operator (the admin_users.id) and is OPTIONAL — absent when the change is performed via break-glass (which has no operator identity). tenant_id is the TARGET tenant whose config changed (a real tenant UUID — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID); team_id = project_id = WILDCARD_UUID (tenant-level action). R6 — MUST NOT carry any secret material: NO client_secret, NO private keys, NO ciphertext. Only the config id + protocol metadata may ride; keep it minimal. protocol is the OPTIONAL protocol slug ('oidc' or 'saml'); idp_config_id is the OPTIONAL config row id for forensic linkage.

protocol.description: OPTIONAL IdP protocol the changed config uses. Protocol metadata only — NEVER a secret, key, or ciphertext (R6).

idp_config_id.description: OPTIONAL id of the changed idp_config row (forensic linkage). NEVER secret material; the OIDC client_secret and any SP private key are encrypted at rest and are NEVER carried on this event (R6).

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (IdP-config change meta-audit record; no secret material carried).

BatchSubmittedEvent.description: Emitted by F-015 (ADR-0018 §8) when an async bulk batch is accepted at submit. A lifecycle/outcome meta-event layered on top of the reused F-005/F-008 per-file path (per-file security findings stay on the existing pii_blocked / injection_detected / secret_leaked / policy_decision_* variants with the tenant's own four IDs). Honest attribution: agent_id is the reserved slug 'bulk-worker' (the async bulk-pipeline principal); tenant_id is ALWAYS the real submitting tenant — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID (a batch belongs to a real tenant); team_id/project_id are the submitting key's REAL IDs (known at submit). Metadata only — NO payloads, prompt content, PII, or secrets.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (batch-lifecycle meta-audit record).

BatchFileProcessedEvent.description: Emitted by F-015 (ADR-0018 §8) when a single file in a bulk batch completes (allowed/redacted) through the reused F-005/F-008 per-file path. A lifecycle/outcome meta-event (per-file security findings stay on the existing pii_blocked / injection_detected / secret_leaked / policy_decision_* variants with the tenant's own four IDs). Honest attribution: agent_id is the reserved slug 'bulk-worker'; tenant_id is ALWAYS the real submitting tenant — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID; team_id/project_id are the submitting key's REAL IDs. Metadata only — NO payloads, prompt content, PII, or secrets.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (per-file completion meta-audit record).

BatchFileBlockedEvent.description: Emitted by F-015 (ADR-0018 §8) when a single file in a bulk batch is blocked by a detector/policy on the reused F-005/F-008 per-file path. A lifecycle/outcome meta-event (the underlying security finding is also emitted on the existing pii_blocked / injection_detected / secret_leaked / policy_decision_* variants with the tenant's own four IDs). Honest attribution: agent_id is the reserved slug 'bulk-worker'; tenant_id is ALWAYS the real submitting tenant — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID; team_id/project_id are the submitting key's REAL IDs. Metadata only — NO payloads, prompt content, PII, or secrets.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'blocked' (the file was blocked by a detector/policy on the reused per-file path).

BatchFileDeadLetteredEvent.description: Emitted by F-015 (ADR-0018 §8) when a single file in a bulk batch is dead-lettered after exhausting its bounded retries. A lifecycle/outcome meta-event. Honest attribution: agent_id is the reserved slug 'bulk-worker'; tenant_id is ALWAYS the real submitting tenant — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID; team_id/project_id are the submitting key's REAL IDs. Metadata only — NO payloads, prompt content, PII, or secrets.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (per-file dead-letter meta-audit record after bounded retries).

BatchCompletedEvent.description: Emitted by F-015 (ADR-0018 §8) when all files in a bulk batch reach a terminal state. A lifecycle/outcome meta-event. Honest attribution: agent_id is the reserved slug 'bulk-worker'; tenant_id is ALWAYS the real submitting tenant — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID; team_id/project_id are the submitting key's REAL IDs. Metadata only — NO payloads, prompt content, PII, or secrets.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (batch-completion meta-audit record).

CodeScanPassedEvent.description: Emitted by F-016 (ADR-0019 §10) when the post-response code-scanning detector ran over fenced code blocks extracted from an LLM response and aggregated a PASS verdict (no finding crossed any tenant threshold). agent_id is the reserved slug 'code-scan'; tenant_id is ALWAYS the caller's real tenant — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID; team_id/project_id are the caller's REAL IDs. HARD RULE: metadata only — this event NEVER carries the scanned code content and NEVER a scanner stack trace.

verdict.description: Aggregated code-scan verdict. On this variant the verdict is 'pass'.

language.description: Language tag of the scanned fenced code block(s) (e.g. 'python'). Bounded; a coarse label, never the code.

finding_count.description: Count of normalized findings the scanners reported across the scanned blocks. A count only — never the finding text or the offending code.

top_severity.description: OPTIONAL on this PASS variant. Severity of the highest-severity finding that contributed to the verdict. OMITTED when finding_count == 0 (a clean PASS has no finding and therefore no top severity); present (lowercase enum) when finding_count > 0 (findings existed but none crossed a tenant threshold). On CodeScanWarnedEvent and CodeScanBlockedEvent it stays required (those verdicts always have at least one contributing finding).

scanner.description: Scanner identifier that produced the verdict (e.g. 'semgrep', 'bandit', 'aggregate'). Bounded; never a credential, never a path.

CodeScanWarnedEvent.description: Emitted by F-016 (ADR-0019 §10) when the post-response code-scanning detector aggregated a WARN verdict (audit-only; no response effect). Also carries the honest streamed would-block case: when a streamed response produces findings that WOULD cross the BLOCK threshold but cannot be blocked because the bytes are already on the wire, the optional 'block_suppressed_by_streaming' field is set true (ADR-0019 §4). agent_id is the reserved slug 'code-scan'; tenant_id is ALWAYS the caller's real tenant — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID; team_id/project_id are the caller's REAL IDs. HARD RULE: metadata only — NEVER the scanned code content, NEVER a scanner stack trace.

verdict.description: Aggregated code-scan verdict. On this variant the verdict is 'warn' (audit-only; includes the streamed would-block case).

language.description: Language tag of the scanned fenced code block(s) (e.g. 'python'). Bounded; a coarse label, never the code.

finding_count.description: Count of normalized findings the scanners reported across the scanned blocks. A count only — never the finding text or the offending code.

top_severity.description: Severity of the highest-severity finding that contributed to the verdict.

scanner.description: Scanner identifier that produced the verdict (e.g. 'semgrep', 'bandit', 'aggregate'). Bounded; never a credential, never a path.

block_suppressed_by_streaming.description: OPTIONAL. When true, this WARN records a verdict that WOULD have crossed the BLOCK threshold but could NOT block because the response was streamed (the bytes were already committed to the client before a complete code block existed to scan). Honest observability for the streamed would-block case (ADR-0019 §4); no false block is claimed. Present ONLY on this variant.

CodeScanBlockedEvent.description: Emitted by F-016 (ADR-0019 §10) when the post-response code-scanning detector aggregated a BLOCK verdict on a NON-STREAMED response and the response body was rejected. The client-facing rejection reuses the existing Error envelope error_code 'policy_blocked' (403) — NO new error_code is introduced (ADR-0019 §8); precise attribution lives in this audit event. agent_id is the reserved slug 'code-scan'; tenant_id is ALWAYS the caller's real tenant — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID; team_id/project_id are the caller's REAL IDs. HARD RULE: metadata only — NEVER the scanned code content, NEVER a scanner stack trace.

verdict.description: Aggregated code-scan verdict. On this variant the verdict is 'block' (non-streamed response rejected via the existing policy_blocked envelope).

language.description: Language tag of the scanned fenced code block(s) (e.g. 'python'). Bounded; a coarse label, never the code.

finding_count.description: Count of normalized findings the scanners reported across the scanned blocks. A count only — never the finding text or the offending code.

top_severity.description: Severity of the highest-severity finding that contributed to the verdict.

scanner.description: Scanner identifier that produced the verdict (e.g. 'semgrep', 'bandit', 'aggregate'). Bounded; never a credential, never a path.

CodeScanErrorEvent.description: Emitted by F-016 (ADR-0019 §10) when a scanner errored, timed out, crashed, produced unparseable or oversized output, or the response was too large to scan in full. The detector FAILS SAFE TO WARN (no response effect, no PASS, no BLOCK) and records this distinct error event (ADR-0019 §6). agent_id is the reserved slug 'code-scan'; tenant_id is ALWAYS the caller's real tenant — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID; team_id/project_id are the caller's REAL IDs. HARD RULE: error_class is a BOUNDED classification label ONLY — this event NEVER carries a scanner stack trace and NEVER the offending code content.

scanner.description: Scanner identifier that errored (e.g. 'semgrep', 'bandit', 'aggregate'). Bounded; never a credential, never a path.

error_class.description: Bounded classification of the failure (e.g. 'timeout', 'subprocess_crash', 'output_too_large', 'response_too_large', 'scanner_missing'). A coarse label ONLY — NEVER a stack trace, NEVER the offending code.

FieldLockedEvent.description: Emitted by F-017 (ADR-0020 §10) when the post-response JSON data-lock detector evaluated a lock rule against a field in an LLM response and WITHHELD that field's value because its release condition was not yet met (e.g. a 'time' lock whose unlock_at is in the future). The field value is removed/blocked from the response; precise attribution lives in this audit event. agent_id is the reserved slug 'data-lock'; tenant_id is ALWAYS the caller's real tenant — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID; team_id/project_id are the caller's REAL IDs. HARD RULE: metadata only — this event carries the locked field PATH (pattern_name) and the condition KIND (violation_type), and NEVER the locked field VALUE and NEVER a response payload.

pattern_name.description: The locked field's dotted path (e.g. 'result.ssn', 'a.b[].c'). A field LOCATION only — NEVER the field value, NEVER any response content.

violation_type.description: The lock condition kind whose criterion was not met (e.g. 'time', 'permission'). Charset restricted to [A-Za-z0-9._-] to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense). NEVER the field value.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'blocked' (the field value was withheld from the response).

FieldUnlockedEvent.description: Emitted by F-017 (ADR-0020 §10) when the post-response JSON data-lock detector evaluated a lock rule against a matched field and its release condition WAS met (e.g. a 'time' lock whose unlock_at has passed, or a 'permission' allow-list that matched the caller), so the field value was RELEASED unchanged. Audit-only: the response passes unchanged. agent_id is the reserved slug 'data-lock'; tenant_id is ALWAYS the caller's real tenant — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID; team_id/project_id are the caller's REAL IDs. HARD RULE: metadata only — this event carries the field PATH (pattern_name) and the condition KIND (violation_type), and NEVER the field VALUE and NEVER a response payload.

pattern_name.description: The matched field's dotted path (e.g. 'result.ssn', 'a.b[].c'). A field LOCATION only — NEVER the field value, NEVER any response content.

violation_type.description: The lock condition kind whose criterion was met and released the field (e.g. 'time', 'permission'). Charset restricted to [A-Za-z0-9._-] to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense). NEVER the field value.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'logged' (the condition was met; the field value was released unchanged).

LockConditionDeniedEvent.description: Emitted by F-017 (ADR-0020 §10) when the post-response JSON data-lock detector evaluated a 'permission' lock rule and the allow-list did NOT match the caller's scope (team_id / project_id / agent_id), so the matched field's value was WITHHELD. Distinguishes a permission denial from a not-yet-time lock (field_locked). agent_id is the reserved slug 'data-lock'; tenant_id is ALWAYS the caller's real tenant — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID; team_id/project_id are the caller's REAL IDs. HARD RULE: metadata only — this event carries the field PATH (pattern_name) and the condition KIND (violation_type), and NEVER the field VALUE and NEVER the allow-list contents.

pattern_name.description: The matched field's dotted path (e.g. 'result.ssn', 'a.b[].c'). A field LOCATION only — NEVER the field value, NEVER any response content.

violation_type.description: The lock condition kind that denied release; on this variant it is 'permission' (the allow-list did not match the caller). Charset restricted to [A-Za-z0-9._-] to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense). NEVER the field value or the allow-list contents.

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'blocked' (the permission allow-list did not match; the field value was withheld).

DataLockErrorEvent.description: Emitted by F-017 (ADR-0020 §10) when the post-response JSON data-lock detector could NOT evaluate the lock ruleset (e.g. a malformed/unevaluable rule config, a field-path traversal budget exhausted, or a streamed response whose bytes were already on the wire). FAIL-CLOSED (ADR-0020 §6): the whole response is BLOCKED rather than released with an unverifiable lock. agent_id is the reserved slug 'data-lock'; tenant_id is ALWAYS the caller's real tenant — NEVER WILDCARD_UUID; team_id/project_id are the caller's REAL IDs. HARD RULE: violation_type is a BOUNDED fail-closed-reason label ONLY — this event NEVER carries a field VALUE, a response payload, or a stack trace.

pattern_name.description: OPTIONAL locked field's dotted path implicated in the failure, when one is resolvable (e.g. 'result.ssn'). A field LOCATION only — NEVER the field value. OMITTED when no single field is implicated (e.g. a whole-ruleset config error).

violation_type.description: Bounded fail-closed reason (e.g. 'config_error', 'traversal_budget', 'streaming_not_supported'). A coarse label ONLY — NEVER a stack trace, NEVER a field value or response content. Charset restricted to [A-Za-z0-9._-] to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense).

action_taken.description: Disposition: 'blocked' (fail-closed: the ruleset was unevaluable, so the whole response was blocked rather than released unverified).
